download.title: Idols of Ash Download Guide
download.intro: The official download is hosted on itch.io by Leafy Games. The current public build is free to claim and supports Windows and Linux, with a Steam release noted as coming soon.

steps.title: Open the official itch.io page
steps.description: Go to leafygames.itch.io/idols-of-ash and click Download or claim. The listing is pay-what-you-want and currently available at $0.00.

steps.title: Choose the correct build for your system
steps.description: Select the package that matches your platform before extracting. Windows build: IdolsOfAsh_v1_13.zip (117 MB). Linux build: IdolsOfAsh_v1_13_LINUX.tar.xz (94 MB).

steps.title: Extract and launch the game
steps.description: After the download finishes, extract the archive and run the game from the extracted folder. Windows users extract the .zip; Linux users extract the .tar.xz.

steps.title: Understand what you are launching
steps.description: Idols of Ash is a first-person horror climbing game built around descent, rope movement, and evading pursuit. Content warnings include disturbing imagery, mild strobing, and themes of illness.

steps.title: Fix black screen issues with the latest build
steps.description: Version 1.13 adds ANGLE rendering mode for Windows to address crashes and black screens. If you encounter issues, also try launching with --rendering-driver d3d12.

quickTips: Always download from the official itch.io page to get the latest build
Version 1.13 is the recommended build for Windows stability
The game is listed as released and supports full offline play after download
Steam release is confirmed as coming soon per the official site

beginnerGuide.title: Idols of Ash Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Your first goal is simple: reach the bottom alive. The game rewards momentum, clean grapples, and route confidence far more than cautious stop-and-go play.

steps.title: Understand the real objective
steps.description: You are trying to descend through the abyss as fast and cleanly as possible while something hunts you. There is no combat. Survival tools are grappling, movement, positioning, and timing.

steps.title: Build confidence with the grappling hook
steps.description: Hold the grappling hook, swing like a pendulum, and release at a useful angle rather than waiting too long. A roughly 45-degree release gives strong forward distance. Good grapples convert vertical speed into safer lateral motion.

steps.title: Use controlled descent only when you truly need it
steps.description: Ctrl descent is safer than a blind drop but much slower than fast traversal. Use it for blind sections or to reset position. Do not rely on it as your default movement plan.

steps.title: Listen to the centipede instead of looking back
steps.description: Audio cues tell you when pressure is rising. Spending too much time checking behind you causes missed anchors and bad falls. Keep your view focused on the next beam, wall, or landing route.

steps.title: Avoid the mistakes that end runs early
steps.description: Most early deaths come from a few repeat errors: grappling straight above yourself during a hard fall, stopping on ledges without a plan, or waiting too long to fire when wind wobble starts affecting aim.

quickTips: Completing Normal Mode unlocks Nightmare Mode
Forward planning is safer than reactive panic
Late grapple releases and straight-down pulls usually cost more time than they save
Every pause on a ledge is a risk if you have not planned the next move

controls.title: Idols of Ash Controls
controls.intro: The core control set for Normal, Nightmare, and First Kiln. Version 1.12 added sensitivity options for mouse and gamepad plus an Invert Y toggle.

rows.notes: Used to line up grapples and landing routes
Fire and maintain the grapple connection
Ledges, route changes, and chained movement
Adds speed during ground movement and setup
Slow rappel for safer drops when visibility is poor

firstKiln.title: Idols of Ash First Kiln Guide
firstKiln.intro: First Kiln is the sharpest difficulty jump in the game. It uses a secret map, ember collection mechanics, and up to four centipedes at the same time.

items.title: Unlock Requirement
items.content: First Kiln opens after you complete Nightmare Mode, which itself unlocks after a Normal clear. This places First Kiln firmly in post-clear challenge content.

items.title: What Makes First Kiln Different
items.content: This mode uses a bespoke secret map rather than the standard abyss route. You can be hunted by up to four centipedes simultaneously. You must collect Embers instead of only focusing on a straight descent. High-pressure areas like the Viper's Pit demand routing decisions that do not exist in earlier modes.

items.title: Core Route Strategy
items.content: Straight-down panic play stops working here. Pull the swarm toward the center before rotating to the perimeter. Collect embers during lateral movement instead of stopping for them. Use the map edges to create breathing room before re-entering danger zones.

items.title: The Biggest Run Killers
items.content: Most failed attempts come from treating First Kiln like a faster Nightmare run instead of a new routing problem. Stopping to line up ember pickups, losing swing momentum in tight terrain, and getting trapped in dead-end anchor geometry are the main culprits.

items.title: Why the Latest Build Helps
items.content: Version 1.1 adjusted rope swinging at greater depths specifically to help with deeper First Kiln sections. If you are practicing the mode seriously, use the newest public build from the official itch.io page.

nightmareModeGuide.title: Idols of Ash Nightmare Mode Guide
nightmareModeGuide.intro: Nightmare Mode unlocks after a Normal clear. The bottom is still the goal, but the run becomes darker, faster, and far less forgiving — demanding route memory, cleaner grapples, and much stronger movement discipline.

items.title: Unlock Requirement
items.text: Finish Normal Mode once to unlock Nightmare Mode.

items.title: Enemy Pressure
items.text: Nightmare uses a faster, more aggressive centipede. The Version 1.11 hotfix specifically restored stronger monster pressure after an update made it too passive.

items.title: Reduced Visibility
items.text: The environment is significantly darker than Normal, so clean recognition of anchor points and landing lines matters much more.

items.title: Movement Discipline
items.text: Slow Ctrl descents are much less practical here. Nightmare is built around committed free-falls, quick swings, and fast releases instead of careful hanging.

items.title: Core Survival Rule
items.text: Do not stop on ledges unless absolutely necessary. Nightmare punishes hesitation much earlier than a first Normal clear.

items.title: Next Unlock
items.text: Clearing Nightmare Mode unlocks First Kiln — the game's final challenge mode featuring ember collection and up to four centipedes.

walkthrough.title: Idols of Ash Walkthrough
walkthrough.intro: A first clear is less about combat skill and more about staying in motion, preserving your line, and refusing to panic when the abyss opens up. The cleanest route keeps your swing rhythm intact and avoids long pauses.

steps.title: Move through the opening fast
steps.description: Treat the opening stretch as setup, not safety. The game teaches basic descent here, but strong runs start by building pace early instead of lingering.

steps.title: Use angled swings to build the run
steps.description: Anchor to beams, rocks, and outcroppings, then release around a 45-degree arc to turn vertical speed into forward movement. This is the baseline route logic for the whole descent.

steps.title: Save rappelling for blind drops only
steps.description: Ctrl descent is the emergency tool, not the default route. Use it when a drop is unreadable, then return to falling and swinging as soon as the landing line is clear.

steps.title: Push through the gate and aqueduct cleanly
steps.description: The tower-gate section and aqueduct are memorable landmarks. The aqueduct gives progress, but hook footholds are tighter there and punish messy aim.

steps.title: Use sound to survive mid-run choke points
steps.description: Track the centipede with audio instead of turning around. Looking back breaks flow, wastes time, and often causes missed anchors right when the route narrows.

steps.title: Commit to the final race to the bottom
steps.description: Late in the run, momentum is the best recovery tool. Small mistakes can still be saved, but stopping on ledges usually gives the centipede the window it needs.

endings.title: Idols of Ash Endings
endings.intro: Idols of Ash centers its finish states around three clear completion paths. The game escalates through Normal, Nightmare, and First Kiln — each changing the pressure before the ending rather than replacing the core identity of the game.

items.question: How many endings does Idols of Ash have?
items.answer: Three endings are documented in full-run videos: a Normal ending, a Nightmare ending, and a First Kiln ending — each reached by completing the corresponding mode.

items.question: What is the Idols of Ash ending unlock order?
items.answer: Complete Normal Mode to unlock Nightmare Mode, then complete Nightmare Mode to unlock First Kiln. The endings follow this same progression order.

items.question: What changes before the Idols of Ash Nightmare ending?
items.answer: Nightmare keeps the same bottom-reaching objective as Normal, but makes the run darker and far more aggressive — so reaching the ending requires a much harsher execution test.

items.question: What makes the Idols of Ash First Kiln ending different?
items.answer: First Kiln is a secret challenge map with ember collection and up to four centipedes at once. Its ending path is built around a different level structure and a much higher mechanical ceiling.

items.question: Which Idols of Ash ending should players aim for first?
items.answer: The practical progression is Normal first, Nightmare second, and First Kiln last. That order matches both the unlock chain and the way the game teaches mastery across all three routes.

grapplingHookGuide.title: Idols of Ash Grappling Hook Guide
grapplingHookGuide.intro: The hook is the whole game. Once the rope starts making sense, the descent stops feeling random and starts feeling readable. Aim with the mouse and hold Left Click to fire — the hard part is angle choice and release timing.

steps.title: Keep your eyes on the anchor
steps.description: The grapple works best when you are actively looking at the anchor point. Looking away while tethered destabilizes the rope and makes falls much more likely.

steps.title: Fire at an angle, not straight above
steps.description: Straight-up panic grapples are the main cause of whiplash damage. A diagonal anchor creates a real swing arc and gives the rope space to convert speed safely.

steps.title: Release around the 45-degree point
steps.description: The cleanest distance comes from releasing near a 45-degree swing angle. That timing turns downward speed into useful forward momentum.

steps.title: Use Ctrl descent as a rescue tool only
steps.description: Controlled descent is best for blind drops and emergency corrections. Relying on it too often kills your pace and leaves you exposed to the centipede.

steps.title: Compensate for wind wobble at depth
steps.description: Long falls make aim drift. Fire the hook at closer targets when the reticle starts wobbling instead of forcing a long, desperate tether.

steps.title: Use quick visual snaps, not long looks
steps.description: A strong hook rhythm is: scan, aim, fire, confirm the tether, then flick up briefly to check the centipede before returning to the rope line.

steps.title: Account for the Version 1.1 rope patch
steps.description: Version 1.1 adjusted rope swinging at greater depths, especially for First Kiln play. Current runs are built around this updated hook behavior — older guides may not reflect it.

centipede.title: Idols of Ash Centipede Guide
centipede.subtitle: This page explains the creature's behavior, the sound cues that matter, and the movement habits that keep your descent alive.
centipede.intro: The Centipede is the game's defining pressure system. It chases in real time, punishes hesitation, and is easiest to read through sound and route discipline rather than constant backward checking.

cards.title: What the monster is
cards.body: The main threat is a giant multi-segmented centipede that hunts the player through the abyss. Community nicknames include Jerry and Murderpede.
bullets: Traverses walls, ceilings, and support beams
Acts as the core pressure mechanic for every descent
Appears across Normal, Nightmare, and First Kiln variants

cards.title: Audio cues to track
cards.body: Sound is the fastest way to judge danger. Chittering, clicking, and scraping sounds are the most important warning signals during a descent.
bullets: Chittering usually means the creature is closing in
Scraping and clicking help you judge proximity without turning around
Listening forward is usually safer than staring backward

cards.title: How it actually catches you
cards.body: The Centipede does not just trail behind. It follows geometry the player cannot use and can pressure chokepoints before you settle into a safe rhythm.
bullets: Real-time pursuit behavior is emphasized in guide coverage
Body segments trail behind the head, so space stays dangerous even after it passes
Contact drains health rapidly, giving only a brief escape window

cards.title: Best evasion habits
cards.body: Winning is more about flow than fighting. Players survive by keeping downward momentum, choosing angle-based grapples, and treating pauses as danger.
bullets: Keep moving instead of lingering on ledges
Use angled grapples instead of panic-hooking straight up
Use slow Ctrl descents only when the drop is truly blind

cards.title: Patch behavior to know
cards.body: The official v1.11 hotfix specifically addressed a monster behavior issue and restored the intended pressure of the chase.
bullets: Hotfix note: the previous update made the monster too shy
v1.11 restored intended pursuit behavior
Post-hotfix runs feel less forgiving when you stall

blackScreenFix.title: Idols of Ash Black Screen Fix
blackScreenFix.subtitle: This module brings the official fixes together so players can get back into the descent without digging through multiple posts.
blackScreenFix.intro: Idols of Ash received rapid early hotfixes. The most important fixes cover Windows rendering compatibility, control settings, and common install mistakes caused by grabbing the wrong build or launcher path.

items.question: What should I try first if the Idols of Ash game launches to a black screen?
items.answer: Update to version 1.13 first. The official patch adds ANGLE rendering support for Windows, and the install guide specifically points players to ANGLE mode if the screen stays black after the brightness prompt.

items.question: Which Idols of Ash patch addressed graphics-driver compatibility issues?
items.answer: Version 1.13 is the key patch. The official devlog says it affects Windows users and adds ANGLE rendering mode to help resolve crashes or black screens caused by graphics-driver compatibility.

items.question: Did Idols of Ash add sensitivity options?
items.answer: Yes. Version 1.12 added mouse sensitivity, gamepad sensitivity, and an Invert Y option. The same patch also fixed monster bio-luminosity not applying correctly.

items.question: What if itch says there are no compatible files or Idols of Ash will not start?
items.answer: Use the correct archive for your platform. The official install page lists a Windows zip and a Linux tar.xz, and it also says to download directly in a normal browser if the itch desktop app reports no compatible files.

items.question: Where should Idols of Ash players check for the latest official fixes?
items.answer: Start with the official itch.io page and the official devlog entries. The install guide also mirrors the current file names and summarizes the most common launch and rendering issues.

steamRelease.title: Idols of Ash Steam Release
steamRelease.subtitle: Players are mostly asking one simple question: where can I get Idols of Ash right now, and what does Steam soon actually mean?
steamRelease.intro: Right now, Idols of Ash is already live as a downloadable release on itch.io, while the official site keeps the Steam messaging short and simple: the game is coming to Steam soon.

items.value: $3.00 USD or more, currently shown with a 100% off launch sale on itch.io

items.topic: What Steam soon means right now
items.value: The official site confirms future Steam availability, while the playable release currently points users to itch.io

gameModes.title: Idols of Ash Game Modes
gameModes.subtitle: Three escalating modes with clear progression. Compare what actually changes between Normal, Nightmare, and First Kiln before committing.
gameModes.intro: Normal Mode is the core first clear. Nightmare Mode turns the same descent into a much harsher execution test. First Kiln shifts the run into a deeper secret challenge with its own map and mechanics.
tableRows: Reach the bottom of the abyss
Reach the bottom under harsher conditions
Collect Embers and survive the deeper route

modeCards.summary: The foundational run. Learn grapple rhythm, safe releases, and descent pace. Clearing it unlocks Nightmare.
Faster centipede, heavier darkness, less room for slow Ctrl descents. Clearing it unlocks First Kiln.
Secret map, ember collection, and up to four centipedes simultaneously. The game's final and hardest challenge.

patchNotes.title: Idols of Ash Patch Notes
patchNotes.subtitle: Idols of Ash received multiple rapid updates right after release. This page turns the official devlog into a cleaner player-facing changelog.
patchNotes.intro: The launch-week patches focus on stability, control comfort, monster behavior, and deep-run rope handling. Key changes are highlighted so players can quickly see what is relevant to their current build.

items.title: Windows Compatibility Update
items.summary: Adds ANGLE rendering support for Windows to address crashes and black screen startup problems.
bullets: Adds ANGLE rendering mode for Windows
Targets graphics-driver compatibility issues
Helps resolve crashes and black screen on startup

items.title: Control and Readability Update
items.summary: A quality-of-life patch focused on input options and monster readability.
bullets: Adds mouse and gamepad sensitivity controls
Adds Invert Y option for mouse and gamepad
Fixes Monster Bio-Luminosity not applying correctly

items.title: Monster Behavior Hotfix
items.summary: Corrects a monster behavior regression introduced by the previous update.
bullets: Fixes the monster becoming too passive after v1.1
Restores intended chase pressure and aggression
Post-hotfix runs feel correctly punishing when you stall

items.title: Deep Descent Rope Fix
items.summary: Improves rope swinging behavior at greater depths, especially for First Kiln.
bullets: Fixes rope swinging issues deep in the run
Especially helpful in the deeper parts of First Kiln
Current speedrun routes are built around this updated hook behavior

speedrun.title: Idols of Ash Speedrun
speedrun.subtitle: The game's short length and grapple movement make it a natural speedrun target. Benchmark runs and route notes for Normal, Nightmare, and First Kiln.
speedrun.intro: Idols of Ash speedruns are built around fast vertical commitment, clean grapple saves, and memorizing where you can drop without hesitation. Public benchmark runs show distinct routing styles for each category.
tableRows: Fast center-line descent with minimal hesitation and limited full rappels
Clean reference for baseline route shape and safe aggression
Aggressive free-fall tempo under darker visibility and higher chase pressure
See how routing changes once the mode stops forgiving slow movement
Secret-map routing with ember pressure and heavier punishment for missed lines
Shows how advanced runs shift from pure descent speed into route stability
Attempts, clears, and category-specific practice footage in one place
Good entry point for comparing evolving routes across all categories

storyExplained.title: Idols of Ash Story Explained
storyExplained.subtitle: Idols of Ash tells its story through atmosphere first and explanation second. This page pulls the key signals into one readable interpretation without a giant codex dump.
storyExplained.intro: The game gives players a clear premise but only a thin layer of direct exposition: descend into an ancient place, survive the pursuit, and keep going deeper than seems reasonable. The story works because the environment, the chase, and the ending structure all reinforce the same feeling.

items.content: The official pitch is simple and strong: you descend thousands of meters into a dark and ancient place while something hunts you. There is no combat layer distracting from that setup, so the descent itself becomes the story's main action.

Idols of Ash relies on environmental scale, ash-filled spaces, ruined architecture, sparse interaction points, and relentless pressure from the centipede. Instead of long lore text, it lets movement and atmosphere carry the meaning.

items.section: Why the centipede matters beyond being a monster
items.content: The centipede is both the game's enemy and its pacing engine. It turns every stop into a threat, which makes the journey feel less like exploration and more like a forced descent through a place that does not want you there.

items.section: How to read the three endings
items.content: Three distinct endings are confirmed across Normal, Nightmare, and First Kiln. Read together, they turn the game from a single survival run into a layered structure: first the core descent, then the harsher repeat, then the deeper hidden route where the ash-and-ember imagery comes closest to the surface.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Idols of Ash

questions.question: Is Idols of Ash free to play?
questions.answer: Idols of Ash is pay-what-you-want on itch.io with a $0 minimum, so you can download it for free. A Steam release is confirmed as coming soon.

questions.question: What platforms does Idols of Ash support?
questions.answer: Idols of Ash supports Windows and Linux via the official itch.io page. The official site confirms a Steam release is coming soon. There are no official Android or iOS builds.

questions.question: How do I fix the black screen in Idols of Ash?
questions.answer: Update to version 1.13, which adds ANGLE rendering support for Windows. If the screen stays black after the brightness prompt, switch to ANGLE mode in the launch options. You can also try the --rendering-driver d3d12 launch flag.

questions.question: Does Idols of Ash have multiple endings?
questions.answer: Yes. Idols of Ash has distinct endings for Normal mode, Nightmare mode, and First Kiln. Each path requires different conditions to reach and offers a different conclusion to the descent.

questions.question: What is the Centipede in Idols of Ash?
questions.answer: The Centipede is the main antagonist of Idols of Ash — a giant multi-segmented creature that hunts you in real time as you descend. It traverses walls, ceilings, and support beams, and sound cues like chittering and scraping are your best warning system.

questions.question: Does Idols of Ash support controllers?
questions.answer: Yes. Idols of Ash added gamepad sensitivity and an Invert Y option in version 1.12. Keyboard and mouse is generally recommended for the most responsive controls, but controller play is fully supported.
